name: Sly Stone
description: American musician, singer, guitarist, and record producer (1943–2025)
abstract: Sylvester Stewart (March 15, 1943 – June 9, 2025), better known by his stage name Sly Stone, was an American musician, songwriter and record producer. He was the frontman of Sly and the Family Stone, playing a critical role in the development of psychedelic soul and funk with his pioneering fusion of soul, rock, psychedelia, and gospel i…

works_created.t: I Want to Take You Higher
I Want to Take You Higher

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I was up there one night and this band comes in unannounced... and then all of the sudden I hear [sings “Sing A Simple Song!”] and it was Sly and they were trying out just going around places... and so I heard this music before it came out on the record by accident.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: Miles Davis plays Sly Stone's record 'Fresh' for the interviewee and says 'That’s what we’re playing,' but the interviewee does not state he met, worked with, or collaborated with Sly Stone directly.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ramsey Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: Maurice White... said, 'We open for' – they were in Philadelphia at the Spectrum or whatever, the big 20,000-seater, and they opened for Sly [Stone].
about_quotes.doc_title: Ramsey Lewis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: I think Miles went to hang out with Sly Stone... They got to hanging out. They got to talking.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Cobb — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
